compositional_truth_check.notes: Registry P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or non-viable. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable living specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: Pot is present but contains no visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Confirmed: Pothos specimen present, anchored by white rabbit figurine.
inventory_reconciliation.p4_shared_black_pot: Systemic Loss: Merged with P2 status; no viable plant growth detected.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.p3: Single-leaf Pothos cutting; appears chlorotic/pale, showing signs of potential nutrient deficiency or light stress.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, potentially compacted. No fungal blooms observed.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of electronic components (header pins) near the pots; no organic debris other than soil particles.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The Pothos (P3) has remained largely static in position. The surrounding pots (P1, P2, P4) have remained devoid of visible foliage throughout the 5-day observation window. No growth or decay progression is noted, suggesting a state of stasis or dormancy.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Fair/Poor. The leaf is pale, indicating chlorophyll degradation or lack of sufficient light for photosynthesis. The lack of new growth suggests a stagnant root system.

anomalies: Introduction of electronic header pins on the desk surface (T-3 to Current).
Presence of crushed eggshells in P3 soil (likely a calcium amendment intervention).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of severe depletion. Only one Pothos leaf remains in the P3 pot. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively empty, representing a total systemic loss of the registered species. The Pothos leaf shows signs of chlorosis. The presence of electronic components suggests an experimental or non-botanical use of the desk space.
